weapons.name: Amon Ram, the Creator
weapons.description: Amon, ram-headed god of creation, appeared to Lady Skover in a vision. No one really believed her story at the time, but it did make the Rams appear more legitimate.

weapons.name: Apprentice's Grimoire
weapons.description: At one point, this was an Expert's Grimoire, but all the advanced pages are damaged beyond recognition.

shops.note: Rare Level 20 weapon added in the 29/05/2026 patch notes.

weapons.name: Beefury, Blessed Blade of the Farseeker
weapons.description: There is a metaphor at play here: you can see through the blade, but the blade can cut through you. This is the kind of poetry the Apix clan enjoys.

weapons.name: Book of Mi'Mizan
weapons.description: Mizan is known for travelling around Merops to learn Naya magic from the Nepsids. No one knows what he did there, but they're no longer taking apprentices.

weapons.name: Cheese Moon
weapons.description: Forged in the depths of Irukal, this axe was designed by a people who had never seen a moon in their life, and it shows.

weapons.name: Clawdius
weapons.description: At the end of a warrior's arms, these claws look like the gluttonous mouths of two giant snakes.

weapons.name: Crabgantua's Kneecap
weapons.description: A creature with three pairs of legs should have more than one kneecap. Crabgantua only had one, but its size and hardness is admirable.

weapons.name: Credence
weapons.description: A bow is an object of faith and each arrow is a mystery. Will it hit its target? Will its target hit back? Is there still time for a second shot?

shops.note: Rare Level 20 weapon added in the 29/05/2026 patch notes.

weapons.name: Dominion
weapons.description: The best defence is sometimes believed to be attack. Whoever said that has never been hit in the face with a steel shield.

shops.note: Rare Level 20 weapon added in the 29/05/2026 patch notes.

weapons.name: Flame of Argol
weapons.description: Burning since before the war of the Four Hundred, this candle is widely regarded as the living soul of the legendary Argol Konda.

weapons.name: Ghost Clams of the Low Tide
weapons.description: Before Lord Rulean stole this artifact from the Nepsids, they thought it was responsible for the tides. Luckily, the theft proved that it was in fact useless.

weapons.name: Glory
weapons.description: To conquer without risk is to triumph without glory. Thankfully, going to battle with that sword makes such a situation very unlikely.

shops.note: Rare Level 20 weapon added in the 29/05/2026 patch notes.

weapons.name: Gorgon Ratsay’s Toothpick
weapons.description: Although the legendary Ratsay used his spear to hunt, cook and eat, this tool was mostly remembered as the first toothpick in Kobold history.

weapons.name: Horns of the Wind
weapons.description: This weapon is a testimony to the Rams' lack of imagination. If something can hurt them, they always picture it wearing a pair of horns.

weapons.name: Ipheion, Star Blossom
weapons.description: Starflowers are pretty common, but few of them are shooting stars. And even fewer can cut an armor in half.

weapons.name: Iron Fins of the Leviathan
weapons.description: Having fins of iron is not necessarily an advantage. In fact, it made the Leviathan slow enough for the fishermen to harvest its precious fins.

weapons.name: Judgement
weapons.description: The institutions of justice are rather uncomplicated in Siagarta. This single axe, for instance, cumulates legislative, judiciary and executive power.

shops.note: Rare Level 20 weapon added in the 29/05/2026 patch notes.

weapons.name: Lady Bee’s Ceremonial Stinger
weapons.description: In the past, Apix princesses ennobled royal guards using their own stingers, but a series of incidents convinced them to use that spear instead.

weapons.name: Large Butterfly Net
weapons.description: This net can be used to try catching world companions.

sources.text: Sold by Wandering Merchant in Tyrna
Sold by Wandering Merchant in Navelin

weapons.name: Light Practice Sword
weapons.description: If you've been given this sword, someone thinks you can't be trusted with a real one.

shops.note: Rare Level 20 weapon added in the 29/05/2026 patch notes.

weapons.name: Magma Mia
weapons.description: According to legend, this is an overcooked slice of pizza from the Primeval Age. Some scholars pretend it is just cheese. Either way, no one ever managed to eat it.

weaponDamage.note: 2H weapon_damage derived from base-attack ratio (live 2H weapons had null damage / no anchor) — verify in-game.

sources.text: Demon faction item; specific drop source not in extracted cdb. Community datamine suggests possible drop from Crimson Citadel (Z2 Valley of Eternal Autumn) or Kobold Mines content — verify in-game.
Demon faction item; specific drop source not in extracted cdb. Community datamine suggests possible drop from Crimson Citadel (Z2 Valley of Eternal Autumn) or Kobold Mines content — verify in-game.

weapons.name: Pocket Hive
weapons.description: It's unclear why these specific bees never evolved like their humanoid sisters, but owning one of these tiny hives has become a status symbol for Apix royalty.

weapons.name: Radiance
weapons.description: Like a sunbeam hitting the dewy blossoms of the morning, this long wooden beam descends vigorously upon the skulls and noses of the unworthy.

shops.note: Rare Level 20 weapon added in the 29/05/2026 patch notes.

weapons.name: Ramulus & Ramus
weapons.description: The purpose of ram horns is to be struck against other horns. For this reason, rams are often represented as twins, so they can strike each other's horns and find purpose in the process.

weapons.name: Rehearsal Scepter
weapons.description: This false scepter is materially identical to a real one. But material reality is of little importance when it comes to rituals.

weapons.name: Rough Shield
weapons.description: This shield is so rudimentary it usually ends up doing more harm than it prevents.

weapons.name: Rusty Knives
weapons.description: These training knives were confiscated from their previous bearer for obvious reasons.

weapons.name: Thornlace
weapons.description: A masterpiece of Apix craft, Thornlace is also an expression of the clan's philosophy: beauty is a warning, always laced with danger.

weapons.name: Twin Fangs of Ratsar
weapons.description: Like most rodents, Ratsar doesn't have fangs, so he had to steal these. Someday, another toothless creature will probably take them from him too.

weapons.name: Twin Pillars of Justice
weapons.description: These weapons have little to do with justice and they certainly aren't pillars, but the Crimson Rams have a taste for metaphors.

weapons.name: Wingsabers
weapons.description: The sudden evolution of the bees' appearance triggered a sort of identity crisis, after which they started making all their stuff look like wings.

sources.text: Demon faction item; specific drop source not in extracted cdb. Community datamine suggests possible drop from Crimson Citadel (Z2 Valley of Eternal Autumn) or Kobold Mines content — verify in-game.
